message.content:
I need to find and read the `XPBleHeartbeatAlive` class in the iOS project at /Users/xpeng/XPMotors/IOS (or /Users/xpeng/XPMotors_IOS). This class contains:
1. A `keepAliveTimer` using `XPGCDTimer scheduledTimerWithTimeInterval:kBleHeartInterval`
2. Logic for tracking system state and background keep-alive duration
3. A `_systemStateQueue` dispatch queue

I need to understand:
- The complete implementation of this class
- What `kBleHeartInterval` is set to
- What system state tracking logic exists in the timer block
- What background keep-alive tracking logic exists
- What other classes/methods interact with `XPBleHeartbeatAlive`
- Where `XPGCDTimer` is defined

Search thoroughly - this might be in the main app, a pod, or a submodule. Report the full file paths and complete content of relevant files.

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture of all the relevant files. Here is a thorough report:

---

## Complete Analysis of `XPBleHeartbeatAlive` and Related Classes

### 📁 File Locations

All source files live under the **submodule** (canonical source) at:
```
/Users/xpeng/XPMotors_IOS/submodule/XPCarControlCore/XPCarControlCore/Sources/Services/DigitalKey/Polling/KeepAlive/
```
Mirror copies exist under `/Users/xpeng/XPMotors_IOS/Pods/XPCarControlCore/…` (the integrated pod).

Files in the directory:
| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `XPBleHeartbeatAlive.h/.m` | **Core subject** — BLE heartbeat keep-alive |
| `XPBLEKeepAlive.h/.m` | Orchestrator — lifecycle → heartbeat |
| `XPBLEKeepAliveBussiness.h/.m` | Business logic — polling switch, iBeacon |
| `XPGCDTimer.h/.m` | GCD-based repeating timer |
| `XPAliveTimeDuration.h/.m` | Background stay-alive duration tracker |
| `XPTrackHelper.h` | System info (CPU/memory/battery) + analytics |
| `XPGeneralAlive.h/.m` | `UIBackgroundTask`-based fallback keep-alive |
| `XPLocalNotification.h/.m` | Local notification helper |

---

### 1. `XPBleHeartbeatAlive` — Complete Implementation

**Header** (`XPBleHeartbeatAlive.h`):
- Exposes a `XPBleHeartbeatState` enum: `.None` / `.Alive` / `.BluetoothNotAvailable`
- Singleton (`+sharedInstance`)
- Methods: `startKeepAlive`, `stopKeepAlive`, `enterBackground`, `becomeActive`, `willSuppend`
- Class method: `+isBlueToothAvailable`

**Key private state** (`XPBleHeartbeatAlive.m` `@interface`):
```objc
dispatch_queue_t _systemStateQueue;         // serial queue for timer
@property XPGCDTimer *keepAliveTimer;       // the 5s GCD timer
@property int sendedBlueIndex;              // last BLE device index sent to
@property NSInteger retrySendedCount;       // retry count on heartbeat failure
@property XPBLEViewModel *bleViewModel;     // BLE send abstraction
@property CFAbsoluteTime backgroundTimeBegin; // when background started
@property float backgroundBatteryLevel;    // battery level at bg entry
@property XPAliveTimeDuration *aliveTimeDuration; // background time tracker
@property BOOL isFirstSendHeartbeatSuccess; // first-heartbeat flag for analytics
@property NSTimeInterval lastSystemStateTime;   // previous timer tick timestamp
@property NSTimeInterval currentSystemStateTime;// current timer tick timestamp
@property NSDictionary *lastSystemInfo;     // system info snapshot (prev tick)
@property NSDictionary *currentSystemInfo;  // system info snapshot (this tick)
@property BOOL isStart;                     // keep-alive running flag
@property BOOL isInbackground;             // app is in background
@property BOOL isWillSuspend;              // app about to suspend
```

---

### 2. `kBleHeartInterval` Value

```objc
#define kBleHeartInterval 5   // seconds
```
The timer fires **every 5 seconds**.

---

### 3. Timer Block — System State Tracking Logic (`initTimerConfig`)

The timer block runs on `_systemStateQueue` (serial) every 5 seconds. It does two things:

#### A. Timer Lag / System Scheduling Anomaly Detection
```
if (!self.isWillSuspend) {
    // First tick: initialise both last and current to now
    // Subsequent ticks: slide the window (last ← current, current ← now)
}
float timerDiff = (currentSystemStateTime - lastSystemStateTime) / 1000.0;
if (timerDiff > kBleHeartInterval + 2)  // i.e. > 7 seconds
    → Log warning: "定时器延迟超过2秒,系统调度可能异常"
    → Send TWO analytics events (event "B069", module "P00004"):
        one with kCompareIndexKey=1 (last snapshot), one with kCompareIndexKey=0 (current snapshot)
        both merged with: VIN, car type, timer duration, seconds since app start
```
This detects if the GCD timer was delayed more than 2 seconds beyond its intended 5s interval — a sign of CPU throttling or iOS scheduling pressure.

**State reset on `willSuppend`:** `lastSystemStateTime`, `currentSystemStateTime`, `lastSystemInfo`, `currentSystemInfo` are all zeroed/nulled so comparisons don't fire spuriously after a wakeup.

#### B. Per-Tick Logging (system health)
- If `isStart == YES` (keep-alive active):  
  Logs memory, free memory, CPU%, battery%, average battery drain rate (per hour), Unity run state, and all `XPAliveTimeDuration` metrics (current/total/continued alive, suspend time, section records).
- If `isStart == NO` (timer running but keep-alive not started):  
  Logs only memory, CPU%, battery%, Unity run state.

#### C. Keep-Alive Heartbeat Dispatch
```objc
if (self.isStart) {
    [self updateTimer:self.keepAliveTimer];
}
```
`updateTimer:` → `sendHeartbeat` → `getNexAvailableBleName:` → `innerSendHeartbeatByBlueIndex:` → `_bleViewModel sendHeartbeatWithBleEcuName:success:failure:`

---

### 4. Background Keep-Alive Tracking Logic

#### Entry/Exit Lifecycle:
| Call | Effect |
|---|---|
| `-enterBackground` | Sets `isInbackground=YES`, records `backgroundTimeBegin` (CFAbsoluteTime) and battery level; calls `[_aliveTimeDuration startRecord]` |
| `-becomeActive` | Sets `isInbackground=NO`; calls `[_aliveTimeDuration stopRecord]` |
| `-willSuppend` | Sets `isWillSuspend=YES`; resets all system-state timestamps to 0 |

#### Per-tick in background:
```objc
- (void)updateTimer:(id)sender {
    [self sendHeartbeat];
    if (self.isInbackground) {
        [_aliveTimeDuration updateTimer];  // tracks sleep/awake segments
    }
}
```

#### Average battery drain calculation:
```objc
- (float)averageBattery {
    float dt = CFAbsoluteTimeGetCurrent() - _backgroundTimeBegin;   // seconds in bg
    float dbl = _backgroundBatteryLevel - currentBattery;           // % consumed
    return dbl * 3600 / dt;                                         // % per hour
}
```
This value is logged every 5 seconds in the heartbeat log.

---

### 5. `XPAliveTimeDuration` — Background Keep-Alive Duration Tracker

**Purpose** (header comment): "caller calls `updateTimer` every 5 seconds; `XPAliveTimeDuration` identifies sleep time windows from timer gaps and records them."

It maintains an array `heartbeatTimeSections` of `CFAbsoluteTime` pairs (start/end of each awake segment):
- `startRecord` → clears array, sets `backgroundTimeBegin`, pushes first timestamp
- `updateTimer` → if gap > **15 seconds** since last update, records a sleep+wakeup pair (the timer was suspended by iOS)
- Outputs:
  - `currentTimeAlive`: time since last wakeup segment began
  - `continuedTimeAlive`: total elapsed since `startRecord`
  - `totalTimeAlive`: sum of all awake segments
  - `totalSuspendTime`: sum of all sleep segments
  - `totalAliveSections`: human-readable timeline of awake segments, e.g. `[0:5:12(10:01:00 ~ 10:06:12), 0:2:05(10:08:00 ~ 10:10:05)]`

---

### 6. `XPGCDTimer` — Definition

**Location:** `…/KeepAlive/XPGCDTimer.h/.m`

A thin wrapper over `dispatch_source_t`:
```objc
// Creates a GCD timer on a given queue
+ (XPGCDTimer *)scheduledTimerWithTimeInterval:(NSTimeInterval)interval
                                       repeats:(BOOL)repeats
                                         queue:(dispatch_queue_t)queue
                                         block:(void (^)(void))block;

// Default convenience: fires on main queue
+ (XPGCDTimer *)scheduledTimerWithTimeInterval:(NSTimeInterval)interval
                                       repeats:(BOOL)repeats
                                         block:(void (^)(void))block;

- (void)invalidate;  // cancels dispatch_source
```

**Key implementation detail:**
```objc
self.timer = dispatch_source_create(DISPATCH_SOURCE_TYPE_TIMER, 0, 0, queue);
dispatch_source_set_timer(self.timer,
    dispatch_time(DISPATCH_TIME_NOW, interval * NSEC_PER_SEC),
    interval * NSEC_PER_SEC,
    0);  // leeway = 0 (no slack tolerance)
```
In `XPBleHeartbeatAlive`, the timer is created on `_systemStateQueue` (a **serial** private queue, `"com.example.timerQueue"`), not the main thread.

---

### 7. Other Classes/Methods That Interact With `XPBleHeartbeatAlive`

#### `XPBLEKeepAlive` (direct orchestrator):
| Call site | What it does |
|---|---|
| `-setUp` | `RACObserve(heartbeat, heartbeatState)` — monitors heartbeat state changes; triggers `willKeepAliveFailBlock` when BLE unavailable + bg time < 10s |
| `-setApplicationInitState:` | If background launch → calls `[XPBleHeartbeatAlive.sharedInstance enterBackground]` |
| `-startKeepAlive` | → `[[XPBleHeartbeatAlive sharedInstance] startKeepAlive]` |
| `-stopKeepAlive` | → `[[XPBleHeartbeatAlive sharedInstance] stopKeepAlive]` |
| `-applicationDidEnterBackground:` | → `[[XPBleHeartbeatAlive sharedInstance] enterBackground]` |
| `-applicationDidBecomeActive:` | → `[[XPBleHeartbeatAlive sharedInstance] becomeActive]` |
| `-notifyAppWillSuspend` | → `[[XPBleHeartbeatAlive sharedInstance] willSuppend]` |

#### `XPBLEKeepAliveBussiness` (policy layer):
- Calls `[XPBLEKeepAlive sharedInstance].keepAliveSwitchOnInBackground = YES/NO` based on Polling mode changes, which cascades to `XPBleHeartbeatAlive`.
- `notifyAppWillSuspend` → `[XPBLEKeepAlive.sharedInstance notifyAppWillSuspend]` → `[XPBleHeartbeatAlive.sharedInstance willSuppend]`
- References `[XPBleHeartbeatAlive isBlueToothAvailable]` in the (commented-out) location hook stats.

---

### 8. Architecture Summary

```
XPBLEKeepAliveBussiness          ← App-level business logic (Polling switch, iBeacon)
        │
        ▼
XPBLEKeepAlive                   ← Lifecycle orchestrator (bg/fg notifications, state machine)
        │  observes heartbeatState via RAC
        ▼
XPBleHeartbeatAlive              ← Core: GCD timer every 5s, sends BLE heartbeat
        │  uses
        ├── XPGCDTimer           ← dispatch_source_t wrapper (serial queue "_systemStateQueue")
        ├── XPAliveTimeDuration  ← Tracks awake/sleep segments (>15s gap = sleep)
        ├── XPBLEViewModel       ← Sends actual BLE heartbeat frames
        └── XPTrackHelper        ← System info (CPU/memory/battery) + analytics
```

**The timer (`kBleHeartInterval = 5`) is always running** once `initTimerConfig` is called in `-init` — even before `startKeepAlive`. It logs system health every tick. The actual BLE heartbeat is only sent when `isStart == YES`.
